claim_boundary: BHSM v5.5 constructs a conditional scale-setting mechanism from the v5.4 action. It fixes a nonzero dimensionless scale branch in terms of symbolic action coefficients, but the absolute unit anchor M_* or ell_* remains open. No measured mass, coupling, W calibration, CKM fit, neutrino limit, cosmological input, rare-B result, or full BHSM completion is claimed.

scale_objects.dimension: length after unit anchor; dimensionless rho before anchoring
scale_objects.name: boundary radius
scale_objects.normalization: R_BH = ell_* rho_0 if an absolute ell_* is supplied
scale_objects.reason: a free radius is not a generated physical scale without a stationary equation and unit anchor

scale_objects.name: Berger squashing
scale_objects.reason: dimensionless anisotropy can order spectra but cannot by itself define eV/GeV units
scale_objects.variation_equation: shape variation not closed in v5.4

scale_objects.dimension: dimensionless coordinate; length only after ell_*
scale_objects.name: collar coordinate
scale_objects.reason: rho can select a branch, but absolute length requires ell_*

scale_objects.dynamical_status: fixed datum for v5.4, optional variation
scale_objects.name: metric normalization
scale_objects.reason: normalized geometry supplies ratios and operators, not an absolute unit

scale_objects.dimension: dimensionless before ell_*; length^-2 after anchoring
scale_objects.name: curvature invariant
scale_objects.reason: dimensionless curvature requires ell_* to become physical curvature

scale_objects.name: measure normalization
scale_objects.reason: normalized volume cannot be re-labeled as mass scale
scale_objects.variation_equation: measure variation contributes to delta_rho equation

scale_objects.action_source: v5.4 scalar_topographic plus v5.5 scale potential
scale_objects.name: scalar/topographic amplitude
scale_objects.reason: the action can select nonzero |sigma_0| symbolically; the absolute M_* anchor remains open

scale_objects.dimension: dimensionless in the reduced scale sector
scale_objects.name: potential coefficients
scale_objects.reason: they determine a dimensionless branch but are not physical units

scale_objects.name: spectral eigenvalues
scale_objects.reason: a dimensionless eigenvalue needs an external/internal scale factor
scale_objects.variation_equation: eigenvalue problem L_hat psi_n = lambda_n_hat psi_n

scale_objects.name: Hessian eigenvalues
scale_objects.reason: Hessian eigenvalues propagate a scale once M_* exists; they do not create M_* alone

scale_objects.dimension: dimensionless running functions plus scale labels
scale_objects.name: RG or scale-field terms
scale_objects.reason: dimensional transmutation is not available without an explicit scale-dependent equation
scale_objects.variation_equation: no closed beta or stationary effective-action equation
